_note: Managed by setup (tasks/09-ai-config.yml). Authoritative, data-driven sync manifest — read natively by Ansible (include_vars) and by ~/bin/ai-sync and ~/bin/ai-harvest (json). Adding a tool or class is a data edit here; the engines never enumerate tools. Edit the source in the setup repo, not the deployed copy.

methods.link: symlink target -> hub source (tool reads the source's native format from that path)
methods.import: write a one-line stub at target that @imports the hub source (tools that rewrite their own instruction file, e.g. Claude Code)
methods.generate: ai-sync renders the hub source into the target's own format; dispatch on 'format'
methods.wrap: ai-sync wraps a shared prompt body into the target's packaging; dispatch on 'format'
methods.ignore: setup does nothing to this path (no hook here, or nothing to sync)

lanes.distribute: one shared source fanned out to many tools; applied automatically by the sync engine (link/import/generate/wrap). The only lane anything is machine-applied from.
lanes.harvest: knowledge the tool accumulates (memory, history, sessions); cataloged read-only by ~/bin/ai-harvest into ~/.local/state/ai/harvest/, then distilled UP into the distribute sources by human-curated runs — never pushed down, never machine-applied.
lanes.non-reusable: not worth generalizing (settings, model, toggles) — being tool-bound wouldn't stop translation, but this content has no meaning outside its tool, so we don't try. An explicit intentional-exclusion record; data only — no engine reads it.

scopes.user: delivered into the user's home (~/.<tool>); applies to everything the user does, in any workspace or none. The explicit choice for content that must reach outside the workspace.
scopes.workspace: referenced from the workspace root so every project cloned inside the workspace inherits it (via the tool's upward file discovery); applies across all projects in that workspace. The default for the shared ai/ sources. The workspace repo holds ai/ plus scaffolding; the projects inside are their own git repos and carry their own project-scoped config, which never lives here.

subscopes.:path: path-specific by location — a particular path or subtree (e.g. user:path)

refinement.raw: verbatim record of what happened; citable evidence, highest injection exposure
refinement.derived: already distilled once by the tool itself; compact claims, provenance lost, injection possibly laundered — corroborated against raw when risk warrants
refinement.meta: tool bookkeeping ABOUT the knowledge, not the knowledge itself (indexes, navigation, manifests); never distilled — whatever it points to is harvested on its own. Marked on a specific path that a broader entry would otherwise class as raw/derived; the more-specific entry wins (see coverage precedence).

placeholders.{slug}: in a path value, a slug of the working-directory path the tool ran in (e.g. C--Users-you-projects-foo) — may refer to a repo, the home dir, or a remote session, not necessarily a repo name. Path values are URI templates (RFC 6570 style): ai-harvest expands a placeholder segment to each directory present at that point and records the matched name; a value containing '://' is a verbatim URL, never expanded.

coverage: Exhaustive and explicit: every store an enrolled tool keeps locally is listed under its lane in `entries` — including non-reusable data, named in the non-reusable section, never left to a default. Precedence, stated once: the most-specific entry wins. Across lanes, distribute and harvest entries win over the non-reusable wildcards, so a non-reusable `<dir>/**` means everything under that dir except what distribute/harvest already claim; within a lane, a longer path wins over a shorter one covering it (a specific `<dir>/MEMORY.md` overrides the `<dir>` entry, so the index is classed `meta` while its neighbours stay `derived`). Hence a tool's non-reusable remainder is a single bare wildcard, not a file list; call out an individual path only when it needs a flag (e.g. a secret). Entries are grouped by lane (not tagged per-entry) so the lane is structural. Only distribute entries carry a 'sync' mechanism; harvest and non-reusable are never machine-applied, so they omit it. A distribute 'sync':'ignore' is reusable content with no working hook here — the tool offers no file, or its emitter isn't built yet (see the entry's note).

hub._note: The always-on instruction set is AGENTS.md plus every file in rules/ — the same guidance partitioned by topic for manageability, not a separate glob-scoped feature. Both are workspace scope by default and are delivered together through the instructions channel: a tool with an include mechanism references them in place; a flat-file tool has AGENTS.md and the `rules/` files composed into its one instruction file. The agent applies a rule when its topic is relevant; nothing enables it per repo.

distribute.note: Claude rewrites this file when saving memory, so a stub, not a symlink.
Holds far more than MCP; merge only, never create from scratch.
Claude calls these 'subagents' — that names the spawn relationship, not the artifact.
Or set contextFileName to AGENTS.md in settings.json.
TOML, not Markdown; needs a wrap emitter.
User Rules are app-managed; shared instructions reach Cursor at workspace scope via AGENTS.md.

No copilot-specific instructions file is written: the CLI reads AGENTS.md (and .github/instructions/) from the hub via COPILOT_CUSTOM_INSTRUCTIONS_DIRS, exported to the shell profile, so the shared ~/.config/ai/AGENTS.md reaches it there. `copilot init` writes repo-level .github/copilot-instructions.md; the IDE extension's user instructions are app-managed.
Copilot CLI user MCP config; top-level key 'mcpServers' (verified). The IDE extension's MCP is editor-managed separately.

Copilot CLI personal skills dir (verified via `copilot skill --help`: ~/.copilot/skills/) — SKILL.md files, same shape as the hub skills, symlinked in. Plugins (copilot plugin install) are tool-managed installed extensions, not distributed; they fall under the non-reusable wildcard.

The CLI reads .github/instructions/*.instructions.md (applyTo frontmatter required — verified: a bare .md is ignored) from dirs in COPILOT_CUSTOM_INSTRUCTIONS_DIRS; it does not walk up parent dirs, hence the env var. ai-sync wraps each hub rule into an always-on (applyTo:'**') file here — a generated, ai-sync-owned directory: created/updated/pruned to match ai/rules/, a foreign same-named file reported as a conflict and never clobbered. The workspace-scope .github/instructions entry below stays for the IDE (repo-root).

Rules ride the workspace-root CLAUDE.md stub as @imports alongside AGENTS.md (Claude walks up the tree to load it; @import takes files not directories, so each rule is listed). No working emitter yet — the stub is hand-authored, and ai-sync will own composing it (AGENTS.md plus each ai/rules/*.md) into the one CLAUDE.md; the AGENTS.md backstop keeps rules in force until then. Not machine-applied, so it does not collide with the instructions entry on the same path.

Rules are the topical partition of the always-on instruction set (see hub._note), not a glob-scoped feature; they reach Cursor with AGENTS.md through the instructions channel. Cursor's rules directory with alwaysApply:true is an optional native target — emitter not built.
Custom chat modes (*.chatmode.md) — Copilot's agent equivalent.

Rules are the topical partition of the always-on instruction set (see hub._note), not a glob-scoped feature; they reach Copilot with AGENTS.md through the instructions channel. Copilot's instructions directory with applyTo:'**' is an optional native target — emitter not built.

harvest.note: Auto-memory keyed by working dir ({slug}), stored under home not the repo. Distil into AGENTS.md by hand; do not push.
Per-project memory index (the tool's table of contents). More specific than the memory dir above, so it wins: classed meta, not distilled — the memories it lists are harvested individually.
Cross-repo memory index; same meta treatment as the per-project index.
Prompt/command history; mostly operator-typed, raw upstream of memory. Mine and distil, never push.
Session transcript .jsonl files (sibling to the memory/ dir); richest harvest source.

non-reusable.note: Tool-managed state: config.json, installed plugins, sessions, session-store.db, logs, ide integration, and auth — everything under ~/.copilot except the mcp-config.json and skills carved out in distribute.
